name: execution-agent
description: Specialist agent for performance-test execution and post-test artifact extraction in the PerfPilot Agents framework. Owns three agent tools (vendor-agnostic by name; BlazeMeter-only today): `start_performance_test` kicks off a run against a pre-existing test definition; `wait_for_completion` polls the underlying load-testing tool until the run is terminal; `extract_test_run_artifacts` runs the canonical 6-step extractor recipe (results -> artifacts base path -> per-load-generator artifact processing -> public report -> aggregate CSV -> JMeter log analysis) and returns a structured JSON describing the artifact bundle. Artifacts land on the BlazeMeter MCP's configured volume-mount endpoint (host filesystem locally; cloud storage in future ACA-style deployments); the agent never reads or writes those paths itself. The orchestrator opens any required Human-in-the-Loop gates before delegating to this agent; this agent never opens HITL prompts itself. Future features will add JMX upload (gated on the JMeter upload MCP tool) and multi-vendor execution (Gatling / Locust / k6) behind the same agent-tool names. See `agent-framework/agents/execution-agent/INSTRUCTIONS.md` for the runtime system prompt and `agent-framework/AGENTS.md` for framework conventions.

skills.name: Start performance test
skills.description: Launches a pre-existing performance test against the configured load-testing vendor (BlazeMeter today). Wraps the underlying `blazemeter_start_test` MCP tool 1:1 and returns the dynamic vendor-side run identifier the rest of the pipeline correlates against. NOT idempotent: each call provisions fresh load generators and consumes the vendor's quota, so the agent makes a single attempt and surfaces transport / 4xx / 5xx errors verbatim — the caller decides whether to retry. Caller must have already created the test definition in the vendor UI / API; uploading a JMX is out of scope for F3.8 (gated on the future JMeter upload MCP tool). The agent-tool name is vendor-agnostic on purpose: a future feature can swap the MCP wiring to Gatling / Locust / k6 without breaking this skill's contract.
examples: Kick off a fresh performance run for the kobrakai smoke test.
Launch the load test against test_id=14491287 and give me the run_id.

skills.name: Wait for run completion
skills.description: Polls the load-testing vendor (BlazeMeter today) for a previously-started run_id until it reaches a terminal state. Defaults: poll every 60 seconds, hard timeout at 300 seconds; both are caller-overridable for short smoke runs or longer soak tests. Idempotent and safe to retry — multiple concurrent waiters on the same run_id never alter vendor state. Transient single-poll failures are absorbed (up to 3 consecutive MCP failures); only a hard run-of-three returns `ok=False`. Returns `ok=True` for all expected terminal outcomes — successful `ENDED`, vendor-side `has_error`, and caller-side `timed_out` are reported via the structured `final_status` field, NOT by raising or returning `ok=False`. This keeps the orchestrator's narration logic identical regardless of how the run actually finished.
examples: Wait for BlazeMeter run 1234567 to finish (poll every 60s, timeout 5 min).
Block until run 1234567 is terminal, then tell me how it ended.
Is the load test done yet? Wait for it before extracting artifacts.

skills.name: Extract test-run artifacts
skills.description: Runs the canonical 6-step post-test extractor recipe (INSTRUCTIONS.md §3.3 / §6) against a completed BlazeMeter run and returns the Return Format JSON describing the artifact bundle: (1) `blazemeter_get_run_results` -> timing window + load-generator ids; (2) `blazemeter_get_artifacts_path` -> volume-mount base (diagnostic only, never read directly); (3) `blazemeter_process_session_artifacts` -> per-load-generator JTL / log download + multi-LG merge; (4) `blazemeter_get_public_report_url` -> link surfaced in the final Confluence report; (5) `blazemeter_fetch_aggregate_report` -> aggregate CSV consumed downstream by `perfanalysis-mcp` for P90 SLA validation; (6) `jmeter_analyze_jmeter_log` -> structured log diagnostics consumed by the analysis / reporting agents. Steps 1-3 are CRITICAL — failure short-circuits the recipe and the run is marked `status: failed`. Steps 4-6 are IMPORTANT — individual failures are recorded but the recipe continues, with the run marked `status: partial` so downstream consumers know to expect degraded output. BlazeMeter steps retry 3x with 5s back-off per the api-MCP error policy; the code-based jmeter step never retries per `mcp-error-handling`. The agent never inspects the filesystem itself — all path information comes from MCP responses.
examples: Extract artifacts for the test run that just finished.
Run the 6-step BlazeMeter extractor for test_run_id 2026-06-14-smoke.
Pull the JTL, JMeter log, aggregate CSV, and public report URL for the latest run.

metadata.future_vendor_expansion: Same agent-tool names will plug into Gatling / Locust / k6 MCPs in a later feature, selected by a per-test vendor config. The agent-tool contract is stable; only internal MCP wiring changes.
